agent_output:
I'll analyze the food delivery domain to design this architecture. Let me first assess the constraints and produce a comprehensive analysis.

**Pre-Design Classification:** `missing_constraints` — Critical NFRs, scale requirements, tech stack, team size, geographic scope, and integration boundaries were not provided. I'll produce a thorough domain analysis and component breakdown, but withhold specific pattern recommendations until constraints are clarified.

```json
{
  "executive_summary": "Food delivery platforms are operationally complex, connecting three distinct user types (customers, restaurants, delivery drivers) with real-time coordination requirements. The domain has well-understood bounded contexts — ordering, fulfillment, logistics, payments, and identity — but architectural decisions (monolith vs microservices, sync vs async, real-time tech choices) depend heavily on scale targets, team size, tech stack, geographic scope, and latency/reliability NFRs that have not been specified. This analysis maps the domain thoroughly and identifies the key architectural decisions that require constraint input before a responsible recommendation can be made.",

  "architecture": {
    "components": [
      { "name": "Customer App / Web", "responsibility": "Browse restaurants, place orders, track delivery, manage payment methods, rate experience", "boundaries": "Read-only access to restaurant catalog; write access to own orders and profile only" },
      { "name": "Restaurant Portal", "responsibility": "Manage menu, pricing, availability, hours; accept/reject orders; signal order ready", "boundaries": "Scoped to own restaurant data; no access to other restaurants or customer PII beyond order details" },
      { "name": "Driver App", "responsibility": "Receive delivery offers, accept/reject, navigate to pickup/dropoff, confirm delivery", "boundaries": "Sees only assigned/available orders; location data flows upstream; no access to payment details" },
      { "name": "Order Service", "responsibility": "Order lifecycle management — creation, validation, state transitions (placed → confirmed → preparing → ready → picked_up → delivered → completed)", "boundaries": "Owns order aggregate; emits domain events on state transitions; coordinates with pricing and inventory" },
      { "name": "Restaurant Catalog Service", "responsibility": "Menu management, restaurant profiles, availability, search and discovery", "boundaries": "Owns restaurant and menu aggregates; provides read APIs to customer-facing apps" },
      { "name": "Dispatch / Logistics Service", "responsibility": "Driver-order matching, route optimization, ETA calculation, real-time tracking", "boundaries": "Owns driver assignment aggregate; consumes order events; publishes location updates" },
      { "name": "Pricing & Promotions Service", "responsibility": "Menu price calculation, delivery fees, surge pricing, promo/coupon application, tax calculation", "boundaries": "Stateless calculation engine with promotion state; no direct order mutation" },
      { "name": "Payment Service", "responsibility": "Payment authorization, capture, refunds, driver/restaurant payouts, ledger", "boundaries": "PCI-scoped boundary; owns payment aggregate; integrates with external payment processor" },
      { "name": "Identity & Auth Service", "responsibility": "User registration, authentication, authorization, role management (customer/restaurant/driver/admin)", "boundaries": "Owns user identity aggregate; issues tokens consumed by all other services" },
      { "name": "Notification Service", "responsibility": "Push notifications, SMS, email across all user types for order updates, promotions, alerts", "boundaries": "Consumes events from other services; owns no business state; integrates with external providers" },
      { "name": "Rating & Review Service", "responsibility": "Post-delivery ratings for restaurants, drivers, and customers; review moderation", "boundaries": "Owns rating aggregate; triggered after order completion" },
      { "name": "Analytics / Reporting", "responsibility": "Business intelligence, operational dashboards, demand forecasting", "boundaries": "Read-only consumer of domain events; no write path to operational services" }
    ],
    "interfaces": [
      { "from": "Customer App", "to": "Order Service", "contract": "Place order, cancel order, get order status", "coupling_type": "synchronous request-response" },
      { "from": "Customer App", "to": "Restaurant Catalog", "contract": "Search restaurants, browse menus, check availability", "coupling_type": "synchronous read" },
      { "from": "Customer App", "to": "Dispatch Service", "contract": "Subscribe to real-time delivery tracking", "coupling_type": "real-time stream (WebSocket/SSE)" },
      { "from": "Order Service", "to": "Pricing Service", "contract": "Calculate order total with fees, taxes, promotions", "coupling_type": "synchronous request-response" },
      { "from": "Order Service", "to": "Payment Service", "contract": "Authorize payment on order placement, capture on delivery", "coupling_type": "synchronous for auth, async for capture" },
      { "from": "Order Service", "to": "Dispatch Service", "contract": "OrderConfirmed event triggers driver matching", "coupling_type": "asynchronous domain event" },
      { "from": "Order Service", "to": "Restaurant Portal", "contract": "New order notification, order state updates", "coupling_type": "asynchronous push + synchronous accept/reject" },
      { "from": "Dispatch Service", "to": "Driver App", "contract": "Delivery offer, route details, status updates", "coupling_type": "real-time push" },
      { "from": "Driver App", "to": "Dispatch Service", "contract": "Location updates, delivery status transitions", "coupling_type": "high-frequency location stream + request-response for status" },
      { "from": "Order Service", "to": "Notification Service", "contract": "Order state change events trigger user notifications", "coupling_type": "asynchronous domain event" },
      { "from": "Order Service", "to": "Rating Service", "contract": "OrderCompleted event enables rating window", "coupling_type": "asynchronous domain event" }
    ],
    "data_flow": "Customer places order → Order Service validates with Pricing Service → Payment Service authorizes funds → Order confirmed event emitted → Restaurant receives and accepts order → Restaurant signals 'ready' → Dispatch Service matches driver via location/availability algorithm → Driver accepts, picks up, delivers → Driver confirms delivery → Payment captured → Settlement queued for restaurant and driver payout → Rating window opens for customer",
    "patterns_used": []
  },

  "domain_model": {
    "bounded_contexts": [
      {
        "name": "Ordering",
        "classification": "core",
        "aggregates": ["Order (root: OrderId — items, status, timestamps, customer ref, restaurant ref)", "Cart (ephemeral pre-order state)"]
      },
      {
        "name": "Logistics & Dispatch",
        "classification": "core",
        "aggregates": ["DeliveryAssignment (root: AssignmentId — driver ref, order ref, route, status)", "DriverAvailability (root: DriverId — location, status, capacity)"]
      },
      {
        "name": "Restaurant Management",
        "classification": "core",
        "aggregates": ["Restaurant (root: RestaurantId — profile, hours, status)", "Menu (root: MenuId — categories, items, modifiers, pricing)", "MenuItemAvailability (tracks real-time stock/86'd items)"]
      },
      {
        "name": "Pricing & Promotions",
        "classification": "supporting",
        "aggregates": ["PricingRule (delivery fee tiers, surge multipliers)", "Promotion (root: PromoId — conditions, discount type, validity)"]
      },
      {
        "name": "Payments & Settlement",
        "classification": "generic",
        "aggregates": ["Payment (root: PaymentId — authorization, capture, refund states)", "Payout (root: PayoutId — restaurant/driver settlement batches)", "Ledger (double-entry transaction log)"]
      },
      {
        "name": "Identity & Access",
        "classification": "generic",
        "aggregates": ["UserAccount (root: UserId — credentials, roles, profile)", "Session/Token"]
      },
      {
        "name": "Ratings & Reviews",
        "classification": "supporting",
        "aggregates": ["Rating (root: RatingId — score, review text, order ref, rater, ratee)"]
      },
      {
        "name": "Notifications",
        "classification": "generic",
        "aggregates": ["NotificationPreference", "NotificationLog"]
      }
    ],
    "context_map": [
      { "upstream": "Ordering", "downstream": "Logistics & Dispatch", "relationship": "Customer-Supplier — Ordering dictates order data shape; Dispatch conforms" },
      { "upstream": "Ordering", "downstream": "Payments & Settlement", "relationship": "Customer-Supplier — Order lifecycle drives payment state transitions" },
      { "upstream": "Restaurant Management", "downstream": "Ordering", "relationship": "Conformist — Ordering reads menu/availability from Restaurant context as-is" },
      { "upstream": "Pricing & Promotions", "downstream": "Ordering", "relationship": "Open Host Service — Pricing exposes calculation API consumed by Ordering" },
      { "upstream": "Identity & Access", "downstream": "All contexts", "relationship": "Published Language — Auth tokens consumed by all; each context maps userId to local role" },
      { "upstream": "Ordering", "downstream": "Ratings & Reviews", "relationship": "Customer-Supplier — OrderCompleted event opens rating window" },
      { "upstream": "Ordering", "downstream": "Notifications", "relationship": "Customer-Supplier — Order events drive notification triggers" },
      { "upstream": "Logistics & Dispatch", "downstream": "Notifications", "relationship": "Customer-Supplier — Delivery status changes drive real-time updates" }
    ]
  },

  "trade_offs": [
    {
      "decision": "Monolith vs Microservices",
      "gains": "Microservices: independent scaling of hot paths (dispatch, tracking), independent deployment, team autonomy. Monolith: simpler operations, easier consistency, faster initial development.",
      "sacrifices": "Microservices: operational complexity, distributed transactions, eventual consistency challenges. Monolith: scaling bottlenecks, deployment coupling, team coordination overhead at scale.",
      "alternatives_considered": ["Modular monolith (recommended starting point if team is small)", "Microservices from day one (appropriate if team > 15 engineers and scale is known)", "Serverless/FaaS for stateless components like pricing and notifications"]
    },
    {
      "decision": "Synchronous vs Asynchronous order flow",
      "gains": "Async (event-driven): better resilience, natural decoupling between ordering/dispatch/payment. Sync: simpler mental model, immediate consistency.",
      "sacrifices": "Async: eventual consistency complexity, harder debugging, need for saga/orchestration. Sync: cascading failures, tight coupling, harder to scale independently.",
      "alternatives_considered": ["Hybrid: sync for user-facing request path, async for backend coordination", "Full event sourcing for order lifecycle", "Orchestration (saga coordinator) vs choreography (event chain)"]
    },
    {
      "decision": "Real-time tracking implementation",
      "gains": "WebSocket: bidirectional, low latency. SSE: simpler, sufficient for one-way updates. Polling: simplest to implement.",
      "sacrifices": "WebSocket: connection management overhead at scale. SSE: no upstream channel. Polling: wasteful, higher latency.",
      "alternatives_considered": ["WebSocket for driver location stream", "SSE for customer-facing tracking", "MQTT for driver fleet (IoT-style protocol, efficient for high-frequency location)"]
    },
    {
      "decision": "Driver matching algorithm complexity",
      "gains": "Simple nearest-driver: fast to build, easy to reason about. Optimization-based: better delivery times, batching, driver utilization.",
      "sacrifices": "Simple: suboptimal assignments, no batching. Complex: harder to debug, needs ML/OR expertise, cold start problem.",
      "alternatives_considered": ["Nearest available driver (MVP)", "Scoring function (distance + rating + acceptance rate)", "Full optimization with batching and route planning"]
    }
  ],

  "flexibility_points": [
    { "area": "Payment processor", "evolution_path": "Abstract behind a payment gateway interface; swap Stripe/Adyen/Braintree without touching order flow" },
    { "area": "Notification channels", "evolution_path": "Plugin-based channel adapters (push, SMS, email, in-app); add new channels without modifying business logic" },
    { "area": "Driver matching algorithm", "evolution_path": "Strategy pattern for matching; swap simple nearest-driver for ML-based optimizer as data accumulates" },
    { "area": "Restaurant catalog search", "evolution_path": "Start with database queries; migrate to dedicated search engine (Elasticsearch/Meilisearch) when catalog grows" },
    { "area": "Pricing engine", "evolution_path": "Rule-based pricing that can evolve to dynamic/ML-based pricing without changing the interface" },
    { "area": "Deployment topology", "evolution_path": "Modular monolith with clear context boundaries can be split into services along those boundaries when scale demands it" }
  ],

  "rigidity_points": [
    { "area": "Order state machine", "reason": "The order lifecycle (placed → confirmed → preparing → ready → picked_up → delivered) is the backbone of the entire platform. Changing states or transitions has cascading effects on payments, notifications, tracking, and analytics. Must be well-defined and versioned." },
    { "area": "Payment authorization/capture flow", "reason": "Two-phase payment (authorize on order, capture on delivery) is fundamental to the business model. PCI compliance requirements add additional rigidity." },
    { "area": "Domain event contracts", "reason": "Events like OrderConfirmed, OrderReady, DeliveryCompleted are consumed by multiple contexts. Schema changes require coordinated evolution (versioned events recommended)." },
    { "area": "Location data pipeline", "reason": "High-frequency driver location updates require a dedicated data path that can handle volume without impacting transactional systems. This pipeline topology is hard to change once established." }
  ],

  "risks": [
    { "risk": "Distributed transaction complexity: order placement spans pricing, payment auth, restaurant confirmation, and driver assignment — partial failures leave inconsistent state", "severity": "critical", "mitigation": "Implement saga pattern with compensating transactions; define clear failure modes (e.g., payment auth fails → cancel order; no driver available within timeout → refund and notify)" },
    { "risk": "Real-time tracking at scale: high-frequency location updates from thousands of concurrent drivers can overwhelm traditional request-response infrastructure", "severity": "high", "mitigation": "Dedicated location ingestion pipeline (separate from business APIs); consider time-series or in-memory store for active deliveries; geospatial indexing for proximity queries" },
    { "risk": "Payment edge cases: customer cancels after restaurant starts preparing; driver picks up wrong order; double-charge on retry", "severity": "high", "mitigation": "Idempotency keys on all payment operations; clear cancellation policy encoded in order state machine; dispute resolution workflow" },
    { "risk": "Restaurant integration reliability: restaurants may have intermittent connectivity or slow response times for order acceptance", "severity": "medium", "mitigation": "Auto-accept timeout with configurable restaurant SLA; offline-capable restaurant app; push notification fallback" },
    { "risk": "Surge pricing fairness and regulatory compliance varies by jurisdiction", "severity": "medium", "mitigation": "Configurable pricing rules per market/jurisdiction; audit trail for all price calculations; cap configuration" },
    { "risk": "Data privacy and compliance: storing customer addresses, payment methods, driver locations across potentially multiple jurisdictions", "severity": "high", "mitigation": "Data residency awareness in storage layer; PII encryption at rest; GDPR/CCPA deletion workflows; minimize PII propagation across context boundaries" }
  ],

  "implementation_guidance": {
    "recommended_approach": "",
    "files_to_create_or_modify": [],
    "patterns_to_follow": "Regardless of final architecture choice: (1) Model order as an explicit state machine with defined transitions and guards. (2) Use domain events for cross-context communication. (3) Separate read and write models for high-read paths like restaurant search and delivery tracking. (4) Idempotency on all write operations. (5) Abstract external integrations (payment, notifications, maps) behind interfaces.",
    "pitfalls_to_avoid": [
      "Starting with microservices before team and scale justify the operational overhead",
      "Sharing a database across bounded contexts — leads to hidden coupling",
      "Synchronous chains across multiple services for order placement — one slow service blocks the whole flow",
      "Storing driver location in the same database as orders — different access patterns and write volumes",
      "Tight coupling between order state and payment state — use events to keep them eventually consistent",
      "Building a custom real-time tracking system when managed services (e.g., AWS IoT, Google Maps Platform) may suffice",
      "Neglecting the restaurant experience — driver and customer apps get attention, but restaurant reliability is critical to the three-sided marketplace"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "missing_constraints",
      "description": "Cannot recommend monolith vs microservices, sync vs async coordination, or specific technology choices without knowing: (1) Expected scale — 1K vs 1M orders/day changes everything. (2) Team size and structure — 3-person startup vs 50-engineer org dictates deployment topology. (3) Geographic scope — single city, national, or international affects data residency, latency requirements, and regulatory needs. (4) Tech stack preferences or existing infrastructure. (5) Latency SLAs — acceptable ETA accuracy, order placement response time. (6) Budget for managed services vs self-hosted. (7) Existing system integrations (POS systems, existing restaurant platforms).",
      "routed_to": "user",
      "options": [
        "Provide scale targets (orders/day, concurrent drivers, restaurant count)",
        "Specify team size and engineering capabilities",
        "Define geographic scope and regulatory requirements",
        "State tech stack preferences or constraints",
        "Define latency and reliability SLAs"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Answer these constraints and I will produce a concrete architecture with specific patterns, technology recommendations, and implementation roadmap."
    }
  ],

  "confidence": {
    "level": 55,
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Bounded context identification — Ordering, Logistics, Restaurant, Payments, Identity are well-established domain boundaries for food delivery",
      "Domain event flow — the order lifecycle and its downstream effects are well-understood",
      "Risk identification — distributed transactions, real-time tracking, and payment edge cases are known challenges in this domain",
      "Aggregate boundaries — Order, Restaurant, Menu, DeliveryAssignment, Payment are natural consistency boundaries"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Deployment topology (monolith vs microservices vs hybrid) — entirely depends on team size and scale",
      "Technology choices (message broker, database, real-time protocol) — depends on team expertise and infrastructure",
      "Driver matching algorithm complexity — depends on market size and optimization goals",
      "Pricing model architecture — depends on business model (commission-based, subscription, hybrid)"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "Three-sided marketplace model (customer, restaurant, driver) — not a vertically integrated model with owned kitchens",
      "On-demand delivery (not scheduled/batch delivery)",
      "Payment collected from customer, split to restaurant and driver (standard commission model)",
      "Mobile-first for customers and drivers, web portal for restaurants",
      "Greenfield build — no legacy system constraints"
    ]
  }
}
```
agent_output_excerpt:
I'll analyze the food delivery domain to design this architecture. Let me first assess the constraints and produce a comprehensive analysis.

**Pre-Design Classification:** `missing_constraints` — Critical NFRs, scale requirements, tech stack, team size, geographic scope, and integration boundaries were not provided. I'll produce a thorough domain analysis and component breakdown, but withhold specific pattern recommendations until constraints are clarified.

```json
{
  "executive_summary": "Fo

content.thinking:
The user wants me to design an architecture for a food delivery platform. Let me classify this first.

Classification: `missing_constraints` — I have no NFRs, scale requirements, integration boundaries, tech stack preferences, or business constraints. The request is very high-level.

Wait, let me reconsider. This is a general architecture design request. I don't have specific constraints like scale, tech stack, deployment environment, etc. But it's a well-known domain. Let me think about whether I should escalate or proceed.

The prompt says: "Do I have enough constraints (NFRs, scale requirements, integration boundaries) to make architecture decisions?" — No, I don't have scale requirements, tech stack, deployment targets, budget, team size, etc. This is a `missing_constraints` situation.
 
I need to apply the strict classification rules here. Since critical information like scale, NFRs, and integration boundaries weren't provided, I should flag this as `missing_constraints` and analyze what's known while highlighting the gaps rather than recommending a specific architecture pattern.

      { "upstream": "Ordering", "downstream": "Ratings & Reviews", "relationship": "Customer-Supplier — OrderCompleted event opens rating window" },

      { "upstream": "Logistics & Dispatch", "downstream": "Notifications", "relationship": "Customer-Supplier — Delivery status changes drive real-time updates" }
    ]
  },

  "trade_offs": [
    {
      "decision": "Monolith vs Microservices",
      "gains": "Microservices: independent scaling of hot paths (dispatch, tracking), independent deployment, team autonomy. Monolith: simpler operations, easier consistency, faster initial development.",

    },
    {

      "gains": "Async (event-driven): better resilience, natural decoupling between ordering/dispatch/payment. Sync: simpler mental model, immediate consistency.",
      "sacrifices": "Async: eventual consistency complexity, harder debugging, need for saga/orchestration. Sync: cascading failures, tight coupling, harder to scale independently.",

    },
    {
      "decision": "Real-time tracking implementation",
      "gains": "WebSocket: bidirectional, low latency. SSE: simpler, sufficient for one-way updates. Polling: simplest to implement.",
      "sacrifices": "WebSocket: connection management overhead at scale. SSE: no upstream channel. Polling: wasteful, higher latency.",
      "alternatives_considered": ["WebSocket for driver location stream", "SSE for customer-facing tracking", "MQTT for driver fleet (IoT-style protocol, efficient for high-frequency location)"]
    },
    {
      "decision": "Driver matching algorithm complexity",
      "gains": "Simple nearest-driver: fast to build, easy to reason about. Optimization-based: better delivery times, batching, driver utilization.",
      "sacrifices": "Simple: suboptimal assignments, no batching. Complex: harder to debug, needs ML/OR expertise, cold start problem.",
      "alternatives_considered": ["Nearest available driver (MVP)", "Scoring function (distance + rating + acceptance rate)", "Full optimization with batching and route planning"]
    }
  ],

  "flexibility_points": [
    { "area": "Payment processor", "evolution_path": "Abstract behind a payment gateway interface; swap Stripe/Adyen/Braintree without touching order flow" },
    { "area": "Notification channels", "evolution_path": "Plugin-based channel adapters (push, SMS, email, in-app); add new channels without modifying business logic" },
    { "area": "Driver matching algorithm", "evolution_path": "Strategy pattern for matching; swap simple nearest-driver for ML-based optimizer as data accumulates" },
    { "area": "Restaurant catalog search", "evolution_path": "Start with database queries; migrate to dedicated search engine (Elasticsearch/Meilisearch) when catalog grows" },
    { "area": "Pricing engine", "evolution_path": "Rule-based pricing that can evolve to dynamic/ML-based pricing without changing the interface" },
    { "area": "Deployment topology", "evolution_path": "Modular monolith with clear context boundaries can be split into services along those boundaries when scale demands it" }
  ],

  "rigidity_points": [
    { "area": "Order state machine", "reason": "The order lifecycle (placed → confirmed → preparing → ready → picked_up → delivered) is the backbone of the entire platform. Changing states or transitions has cascading effects on payments, notifications, tracking, and analytics. Must be well-defined and versioned." },
    { "area": "Payment authorization/capture flow", "reason": "Two-phase payment (authorize on order, capture on delivery) is fundamental to the business model. PCI compliance requirements add additional rigidity." },
    { "area": "Domain event contracts", "reason": "Events like OrderConfirmed, OrderReady, DeliveryCompleted are consumed by multiple contexts. Schema changes require coordinated evolution (versioned events recommended)." },
    { "area": "Location data pipeline", "reason": "High-frequency driver location updates require a dedicated data path that can handle volume without impacting transactional systems. This pipeline topology is hard to change once established." }
  ],

  "risks": [
    { "risk": "Distributed transaction complexity: order placement spans pricing, payment auth, restaurant confirmation, and driver assignment — partial failures leave inconsistent state", "severity": "critical", "mitigation": "Implement saga pattern with compensating transactions; define clear failure modes (e.g., payment auth fails → cancel order; no driver available within timeout → refund and notify)" },
    { "risk": "Real-time tracking at scale: high-frequency location updates from thousands of concurrent drivers can overwhelm traditional request-response infrastructure", "severity": "high", "mitigation": "Dedicated location ingestion pipeline (separate from business APIs); consider time-series or in-memory store for active deliveries; geospatial indexing for proximity queries" },
    { "risk": "Payment edge cases: customer cancels after restaurant starts preparing; driver picks up wrong order; double-charge on retry", "severity": "high", "mitigation": "Idempotency keys on all payment operations; clear cancellation policy encoded in order state machine; dispute resolution workflow" },
    { "risk": "Restaurant integration reliability: restaurants may have intermittent connectivity or slow response times for order acceptance", "severity": "medium", "mitigation": "Auto-accept timeout with configurable restaurant SLA; offline-capable restaurant app; push notification fallback" },
    { "risk": "Surge pricing fairness and regulatory compliance varies by jurisdiction", "severity": "medium", "mitigation": "Configurable pricing rules per market/jurisdiction; audit trail for all price calculations; cap configuration" },
    { "risk": "Data privacy and compliance: storing customer addresses, payment methods, driver locations across potentially multiple jurisdictions", "severity": "high", "mitigation": "Data residency awareness in storage layer; PII encryption at rest; GDPR/CCPA deletion workflows; minimize PII propagation across context boundaries" }
  ],

  "implementation_guidance": {
    "recommended_approach": "",
    "files_to_create_or_modify": [],
    "patterns_to_follow": "Regardless of final architecture choice: (1) Model order as an explicit state machine with defined transitions and guards. (2) Use domain events for cross-context communication. (3) Separate read and write models for high-read paths like restaurant search and delivery tracking. (4) Idempotency on all write operations. (5) Abstract external integrations (payment, notifications, maps) behind interfaces.",
    "pitfalls_to_avoid": [
      "Starting with microservices before team and scale justify the operational overhead",
      "Sharing a database across bounded contexts — leads to hidden coupling",
      "Synchronous chains across multiple services for order placement — one slow service blocks the whole flow",
      "Storing driver location in the same database as orders — different access patterns and write volumes",
      "Tight coupling between order state and payment state — use events to keep them eventually consistent",
      "Building a custom real-time tracking system when managed services (e.g., AWS IoT, Google Maps Platform) may suffice",
      "Neglecting the restaurant experience — driver and customer apps get attention, but restaurant reliability is critical to the three-sided marketplace"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "missing_constraints",
      "description": "Cannot recommend monolith vs microservices, sync vs async coordination, or specific technology choices without knowing: (1) Expected scale — 1K vs 1M orders/day changes everything. (2) Team size and structure — 3-person startup vs 50-engineer org dictates deployment topology. (3) Geographic scope — single city, national, or international affects data residency, latency requirements, and regulatory needs. (4) Tech stack preferences or existing infrastructure. (5) Latency SLAs — acceptable ETA accuracy, order placement response time. (6) Budget for managed services vs self-hosted. (7) Existing system integrations (POS systems, existing restaurant platforms).",
      "routed_to": "user",
      "options": [
        "Provide scale targets (orders/day, concurrent drivers, restaurant count)",
        "Specify team size and engineering capabilities",
        "Define geographic scope and regulatory requirements",
        "State tech stack preferences or constraints",
        "Define latency and reliability SLAs"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Answer these constraints and I will produce a concrete architecture with specific patterns, technology recommendations, and implementation roadmap."
    }
  ],

  "confidence": {
    "level": 55,
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Bounded context identification — Ordering, Logistics, Restaurant, Payments, Identity are well-established domain boundaries for food delivery",
      "Domain event flow — the order lifecycle and its downstream effects are well-understood",
      "Risk identification — distributed transactions, real-time tracking, and payment edge cases are known challenges in this domain",
      "Aggregate boundaries — Order, Restaurant, Menu, DeliveryAssignment, Payment are natural consistency boundaries"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Deployment topology (monolith vs microservices vs hybrid) — entirely depends on team size and scale",
      "Technology choices (message broker, database, real-time protocol) — depends on team expertise and infrastructure",
      "Driver matching algorithm complexity — depends on market size and optimization goals",
      "Pricing model architecture — depends on business model (commission-based, subscription, hybrid)"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "Three-sided marketplace model (customer, restaurant, driver) — not a vertically integrated model with owned kitchens",
      "On-demand delivery (not scheduled/batch delivery)",
      "Payment collected from customer, split to restaurant and driver (standard commission model)",
      "Mobile-first for customers and drivers, web portal for restaurants",
      "Greenfield build — no legacy system constraints"
    ]
  }
}

trace.result:
I'll analyze the food delivery domain to design this architecture. Let me first assess the constraints and produce a comprehensive analysis.

**Pre-Design Classification:** `missing_constraints` — Critical NFRs, scale requirements, tech stack, team size, geographic scope, and integration boundaries were not provided. I'll produce a thorough domain analysis and component breakdown, but withhold specific pattern recommendations until constraints are clarified.

  "executive_summary": "Food delivery platforms are operationally complex, connecting three distinct user types (customers, restaurants, delivery drivers) with real-time coordination requirements. The domain has well-understood bounded contexts — ordering, fulfillment, logistics, payments, and identity — but architectural decisions (monolith vs microservices, sync vs async, real-time tech choices) depend heavily on scale targets, team size, tech stack, geographic scope, and latency/reliability NFRs that have not been specified. This analysis maps the domain thoroughly and identifies the key architectural decisions that require constraint input before a responsible recommendation can be made.",

  "architecture": {
    "components": [

      { "name": "Restaurant Portal", "responsibility": "Manage menu, pricing, availability, hours; accept/reject orders; signal order ready", "boundaries": "Scoped to own restaurant data; no access to other restaurants or customer PII beyond order details" },
      { "name": "Driver App", "responsibility": "Receive delivery offers, accept/reject, navigate to pickup/dropoff, confirm delivery", "boundaries": "Sees only assigned/available orders; location data flows upstream; no access to payment details" },
      { "name": "Order Service", "responsibility": "Order lifecycle management — creation, validation, state transitions (placed → confirmed → preparing → ready → picked_up → delivered → completed)", "boundaries": "Owns order aggregate; emits domain events on state transitions; coordinates with pricing and inventory" },
      { "name": "Restaurant Catalog Service", "responsibility": "Menu management, restaurant profiles, availability, search and discovery", "boundaries": "Owns restaurant and menu aggregates; provides read APIs to customer-facing apps" },
      { "name": "Dispatch / Logistics Service", "responsibility": "Driver-order matching, route optimization, ETA calculation, real-time tracking", "boundaries": "Owns driver assignment aggregate; consumes order events; publishes location updates" },
      { "name": "Pricing & Promotions Service", "responsibility": "Menu price calculation, delivery fees, surge pricing, promo/coupon application, tax calculation", "boundaries": "Stateless calculation engine with promotion state; no direct order mutation" },
      { "name": "Payment Service", "responsibility": "Payment authorization, capture, refunds, driver/restaurant payouts, ledger", "boundaries": "PCI-scoped boundary; owns payment aggregate; integrates with external payment processor" },
      { "name": "Identity & Auth Service", "responsibility": "User registration, authentication, authorization, role management (customer/restaurant/driver/admin)", "boundaries": "Owns user identity aggregate; issues tokens consumed by all other services" },
      { "name": "Notification Service", "responsibility": "Push notifications, SMS, email across all user types for order updates, promotions, alerts", "boundaries": "Consumes events from other services; owns no business state; integrates with external providers" },
      { "name": "Rating & Review Service", "responsibility": "Post-delivery ratings for restaurants, drivers, and customers; review moderation", "boundaries": "Owns rating aggregate; triggered after order completion" },
      { "name": "Analytics / Reporting", "responsibility": "Business intelligence, operational dashboards, demand forecasting", "boundaries": "Read-only consumer of domain events; no write path to operational services" }
    ],

      { "from": "Customer App", "to": "Dispatch Service", "contract": "Subscribe to real-time delivery tracking", "coupling_type": "real-time stream (WebSocket/SSE)" },
      { "from": "Order Service", "to": "Pricing Service", "contract": "Calculate order total with fees, taxes, promotions", "coupling_type": "synchronous request-response" },
      { "from": "Order Service", "to": "Payment Service", "contract": "Authorize payment on order placement, capture on delivery", "coupling_type": "synchronous for auth, async for capture" },
      { "from": "Order Service", "to": "Dispatch Service", "contract": "OrderConfirmed event triggers driver matching", "coupling_type": "asynchronous domain event" },
      { "from": "Order Service", "to": "Restaurant Portal", "contract": "New order notification, order state updates", "coupling_type": "asynchronous push + synchronous accept/reject" },
      { "from": "Dispatch Service", "to": "Driver App", "contract": "Delivery offer, route details, status updates", "coupling_type": "real-time push" },
      { "from": "Driver App", "to": "Dispatch Service", "contract": "Location updates, delivery status transitions", "coupling_type": "high-frequency location stream + request-response for status" },
      { "from": "Order Service", "to": "Notification Service", "contract": "Order state change events trigger user notifications", "coupling_type": "asynchronous domain event" },
      { "from": "Order Service", "to": "Rating Service", "contract": "OrderCompleted event enables rating window", "coupling_type": "asynchronous domain event" }
    ],
